county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Yalobusha County formed 1833 from the Choctaw Cession of 1830. County seat is Water Valley, with Coffeeville as the original seat (two judicial districts). Straddles the Delta edge and hills. Named for the Yalobusha River (Choctaw for 'tadpole place').
county_metadata.research_tips: Yalobusha County (formed 1833 from Choctaw Cession) has Water Valley as county seat, with Coffeeville as the original seat (two judicial districts). The Chancery Clerk holds deed books from 1834 and chancery court records. MDAH has microfilm of Yalobusha County land records, chancery minutes, and marriage books. BLM GLO land patents document Choctaw Cession entries. Yalobusha County straddles the Delta edge and hills. The county had a significant enslaved population in the western portion. Freedmen's Bureau records cover the area. FamilySearch has digitized Yalobusha County clerk records. Baptist and Methodist church records from the 1830s document early families. Tax rolls at MDAH trace landholding patterns. The Skuna River watershed influenced settlement.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: MDAH microfilm of Yalobusha County deed books from 1833; Chancery Clerk in Water Valley maintains originals; BLM GLO federal land patents at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.source_name: Yalobusha County Deed Books -- Chancery Clerk / MDAH
source_profiles.repository: Yalobusha County Chancery Clerk, False / Mississippi Department of Archives and History, Jackson
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books for Yalobusha County from 1833; MDAH holds microfilm copies; Chancery Clerk in False maintains originals; Mississippi is a public land state with BLM GLO patents available online
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1833; original deed books held by Chancery Clerk in False

source_profiles.source_name: Yalobusha County Chancery Court Records (Probate) -- Chancery Clerk / MDAH
source_profiles.repository: Yalobusha County Chancery Clerk, False / Mississippi Department of Archives and History, Jackson
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mississippi uses Chancery Court for probate (unique system -- no separate probate court). Wills, estates, guardianships, and land partitions for Yalobusha County from 1833; MDAH has microfilm of chancery minutes and estate files

source_profiles.source_name: Yalobusha County Marriage Records -- Chancery Clerk / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Yalobusha County Chancery Clerk, False / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Yalobusha County from 1833; FamilySearch has indexed many Mississippi county marriage records; state marriage registration began 1926 through Mississippi State Department of Health

Federal census for Yalobusha County from 1840 through 1950; includes slave schedules 1850-1860 listing enslaved persons by owner; agricultural and manufacturing schedules provide economic data; the 1890 census was destroyed nationally

source_profiles.source_name: Yalobusha County Choctaw Cession and Removal Records -- NARA / BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: NARA (Record Group 75) / Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Yalobusha County was formed from Choctaw Cession lands (Treaty of Dancing Rabbit Creek, 1830). BLM GLO records document federal land sales following removal. NARA Record Group 75 holds Choctaw removal rolls, allotment records, and the Armstrong Roll

source_profiles.source_name: Yalobusha County Military Records -- MDAH / NARA / Fold3
source_profiles.repository: Mississippi Department of Archives and History, Jackson / NARA / Fold3
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil War Confederate service records for Yalobusha County soldiers; Mississippi contributed heavily to the Confederate army; MDAH holds state adjutant general records; NARA has compiled Confederate service records; Fold3 has digitized many; also includes WWI/WWII draft registrations

source_profiles.source_name: Yalobusha County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / MSGenWeb
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / MSGenWeb / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Yalobusha County churches, community cemeteries, and family burial grounds; Find A Grave has growing coverage; MSGenWeb volunteers have transcribed many Yalobusha County cemeteries; particularly valuable when civil records are incomplete or destroyed

county_metadata.record_losses: 1864 Union forces burned courthouse; 1904 fire destroyed additional records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Yazoo County formed 1823 from Hinds County. County seat is Yazoo City. Burned county: 1864 Union forces burned the courthouse; 1904 fire destroyed additional records. Located at the eastern edge of the Delta. Named for the Yazoo River and the Yazoo people.
county_metadata.research_tips: Yazoo County (formed 1823 from Hinds) is a burned county due to 1864 Union forces burning the courthouse and a 1904 fire that destroyed additional records. County seat is Yazoo City. The Chancery Clerk holds surviving and reconstructed records. For the burned periods, use BLM GLO federal land patents (glorecords.blm.gov), MDAH microfilm of surviving materials, and Hinds County records for pre-1823 entries. Yazoo County is at the edge of the Delta with extensive cotton plantations. MDAH has plantation records and estate inventories for Yazoo County. Freedmen's Bureau records for the Yazoo sub-district are essential. The Yazoo County Library has local history materials. FamilySearch has digitized available Yazoo County clerk records. Baptist and Methodist church records from the 1820s-1830s supplement the destroyed civil records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: MDAH microfilm of Yazoo County deed books from 1823; Chancery Clerk in Yazoo City maintains originals; BLM GLO federal land patents at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.survival_notes: Significant losses from 1864 Union forces burned courthouse; reconstructed records and surviving fragments at Chancery Clerk and MDAH
